content.content:
1→# [ASG](https://github.com/Graph-Research-Labs/ASG)
     2→
     3→API Specification Generator (ASG) is a tool that generates OpenAPI specification files from an ontology. The tool is
     4→designed to generate OpenAPI specification files for APIs that are based on ontologies. The tool is designed to be run
     5→from the command line or can be integrated into a java application/maven build.
     6→In turn these specification files can be used to generate API documentation, client and server code, and other tools
     7→that can be used to interact with the API.
     8→
     9→## Prerequisites:
    10→
    11→1. Java 21 or later.
    12→2. Maven 3.6.3 or later.
    13→3. An ontology in OWL RDF format.
    14→4. A configuration file in YAML format.
    15→5. An XML catalog file to resolve ontology imports (optional).
    16→
    17→## Build steps:
    18→
    19→1. `mvn clean package` to compile and package the jars.
    20→2. `mvn clean install` to compile and install jars in your local maven repository.
    21→2. `mvn clean deploy` to deploy the jars to the remote repository.
    22→
    23→The target directory will contain the following jars:
    24→
    25→1. asg-<revision>.jar - The main jar file for use when embedding the tool in a java application.
    26→2. asg-<revision>-jar-with-dependencies.jar - The executable jar file with all dependencies included.
    27→3. asg-<revision>-sources.jar - The source jar file.
    28→
    29→## How to run from the command line:
    30→
    31→1. `java -jar asg-<revision>-jar-with-dependencies.jar -c <config-file>` to run the executable jar file.
    32→
    33→## Jar may be copied from maven repository:
    34→
    35→`mvn dependency:copy -Dartifact=com.grl.component:asg:0.0.4-SNAPSHOT:jar:jar-with-dependencies -DoverWriteIfNewer=true -DoutputDirectory=.`
    36→
    37→## How to run as part of a maven build:
    38→
    39→1. Add the following dependency to your pom.xml file:
    40→
    41→```xml
    42→
    43→<dependency>
    44→    <groupId>com.graphresearchlabs</groupId>
    45→    <artifactId>asg</artifactId>
    46→    <version>0.0.4</version>
    47→</dependency>
    48→```
    49→
    50→2. Add the following plugin to your pom.xml file:
    51→
    52→```xml  
    53→
    54→<groupId>org.codehaus.mojo</groupId>
    55→<artifactId>exec-maven-plugin</artifactId>
    56→<version>1.6.0</version>
    57→<goal>java</goal>
    58→<configuration>
    59→<mainClass>com.grl.component.asg.Asg</mainClass>
    60→<arguments>
    61→    <argument>-c</argument>
    62→    <argument>${path to config}</argument>
    63→</arguments>
    64→</configuration>
    65→```
    66→
    67→## Getting Started
    68→
    69→Briefly:
    70→
    71→1. An ontology must be available as an owl RDF file.
    72→2. A configuration file must be created to specify the output directory, ontology file and configuration options.
    73→3. Run the executable jar file with the configuration file as an argument.
    74→4. OpenApi specification files (yaml and json) will be generated in the output directory.
    75→
    76→## Configuration File
    77→
    78→The yaml file consists of the following fields:
    79→
    80→* `name`: (required) The name of the API. This will be used as a namespace when identifying this API amongst others.
    81→* `ontologies`: (required) A list of ontology file(s)/uri(s). If there are multiple files, they will be merged into a
    82→  single ontology.
    83→* `outputDir`: (required) The directory where the OpenApi specification files will be generated. Note that a
    84→  subdirectory with the name of the API will be created in this directory.
    85→* `rootClass`: (optional) IRI of the root class from which all resources in the API are subclassed with
    86→  hasResourceSchema. If not defined will default to the only single class that is a subclass of APIRoot. If more than
    87→  one, this value is required.
    88→* `extends`: (optional) The path to another configuration file to extend. The fields in the extended configuration file
    89→  will be merged with the fields in the current configuration file. The fields in the current configuration file will
    90→  take precedence. Lists will be concatenated, maps will be merged.
    91→* `namespaceMappings`: (optional) A URIs to be flatten into. This is used to map IRIs to flatten
    92→  single
    93→  namespace. The values are arrays or regexps to match namespaces to be flattened. Regexps may be
    94→  optionally preceded by '!' to specify exclusion
    95→* `catalogFile`: (optional) The location of an XML catalog file to resolve ontology imports.
    96→* `securitySchemaName`: (optional) name of the security scheme to use for the generated api. If not specified, the first
    97→  oauth2 security scheme found in the openapi template will be used
    98→* `enforceRequiredness`" (optional) If true, properties which have minCardinality/exact > 0 or some will be added to
    99→  `required` sets on owning objects in the JSON schema
   100→* `defaultSecurityTemplate` (optional) Template with placeholders that will be used when no security annotations are
   101→  present` to generate security requirements. Placeholders
   102→  are as follows:
   103→* {api} the name of the api (config.name)
   104→  * {action} mapped for each http method being generated from the config.securityActionMappings
   105→  * {model} The resource (model) name for each api resource being generated
   106→    A null or empty defaultSecurityTemplate will suppress generation of any security specification
   107→    for individual paths when no security annotation is present
   108→* `securityTemplate` (optional, defaulted) Template with placeholders that will be used with the security annotations on
   109→  a resource to generate security requirements
   110→  * {security} the security string as obtained from the "security" annotation. Other place holders will be replaced as
   111→    above.
   112→    A null or empty securityTemplate will suppress generation of any security specification for
   113→    individual paths when a security annotations is present
   114→* `templates`: (optional) root of templates as follows:
   115→    * `openApi`: (optional) top level [OpenAPI](https://swagger.io/specification/#openapi-object) which will have
   116→      generated values merged in
   117→    * `collectionPathItem`: (optional) Template the [PathItem](https://swagger.io/specification/#path-item-object) for a
   118→      collection api operations (.g. GET /<resource>). Generated spec will be merged over this for each resource's
   119→      collection operations.
   120→    * `singlePathItem`: (optional) Template the [PathItem](https://swagger.io/specification/#path-item-object) for a
   121→      single resource api operations (.g. GET /<resource>/<id>). Generated spec will be merged over
   122→
   123→All relative paths and uris are interpreted relative to the location of the configuration file they appear in.
   124→
   125→Defaults are read from the src/main/resources/default.config.yaml resource as if this were an implied global 'extends'
   126→config.
   127→
   128→## Example Configuration File
   129→
   130→```yaml
   131→ontologies:
   132→  - ../Insurance Common Data Record API/Insurance Core Data Record API Ontology-1.0.4.owl
   133→name: insuranceCDR
   134→extends: base-config.yaml
   135→catalogFile: ../Insurance Common Data Record API/catalog-v001.xml
   136→namespaceMappings:
   137→  https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/InsuranceCDR/:
   138→    - https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/.*
   139→    - https://ontologies.semanticarts.com/.*
   140→    - https://w3id.org/semanticarts/ns/ontology/gist/.*
   141→rootClass: https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/InsuranceCDR/InsuranceCDRAPIV1.0.0
   142→templates:
   143→  openapi:
   144→    info:
   145→      description: Insurance API
   146→      title: Insurance API
   147→    servers:
   148→      - url: insurance
   149→        description: description
   150→```
   151→
   152→This uses a base-config.yaml to supply some values which would be shared by other configs in the project:
   153→
   154→```yaml
   155→outputDir: target
   156→templates:
   157→  openapi:
   158→    openapi: 3.1.0
   159→    info:
   160→      version: 1.0.1
   161→      termsOfService: terms
   162→      contact:
   163→        name: Dougal Watt
   164→        url: http://www.climatetracker.com/
   165→        email: support@graphresearchlabs.com
   166→      license:
   167→        name: Copyright 2024 Graphic Research Labs
   168→        url: http://www.climatetracker.com/
   169→    components:
   170→      securitySchemes:
   171→        oauth2:
   172→          type: oauth2
   173→          flows:
   174→            authorizationCode:
   175→              authorizationUrl: https://dummy
   176→              tokenUrl: https://dummy
   177→
   178→    externalDocs:
   179→      description: External Documentation
   180→      url: http://www.climatetracker.com/
   181→```
   182→## Example Output
   183→
   184→```terminal
   185→output
   186→├── insuranceCDR
   187→│   ├── effective-config.yaml (merged configuration file, with extends file and paths etc resolved recursively) 
   188→│   ├── ontology0.owl (each ontology file is copied to the output directory)
   189→│   ├── ontology-flat.owl (ontology file with namespace mappings flattened)
   190→│   ├── ontology-merged.owl (ontology file with all imports merged)
   191→│   ├── openapi.json (generated OpenAPI specification file JSON)
   192→│   └── openapi.yaml (generated OpenAPI specification file YAML)
   193→````
   194→
   195→## Ontology design
   196→
   197→To successfully run ASG, an ontology must be supplied that supports the Graph Research Labs annotation and structural ontology requirements. These requirements are applied to an existing ontology, and create a graph view on top of the ontology suitable for generating a YAML specification.
   198→
   199→The process for creating an ASG-ready ontology is as follows:
   200→
   201→Structure the ontology
   202→1. Create your ontology by first importing the Graph Research Labs 'base' ontology. This will provide the minimal ontology structure required for ASG and includes structural classes and properties that are critical and cannot be removed:
   203→- 'API Root' class - this links to a class containing the api specification you will generate with ASG, via the 'has api' object property. This class should be subclassed off 'Specification Entry', and contain as it's URI the shortened named and version number of the API (to allow for multiple versions if required). For example: https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/employment/EmploymentAPIV1.0.1
   204→- the set of object properties used in constructing the API, located as sub-properties of 'api object properties'
   205→  'has api' - links API Root to the API specification to be generated
   206→  'has resource schema' - defines the API resources that will be generated by ASG, as links to one or more classes. For example, " 'has resource schema' exactly 1 'Disclose Climate Related Impacts' " will generate GET, PUT, POST, PATCH and DELETE API resources, and the associated schema elements that are linked as triples from the 'Disclose Climate Related Impacts' class.
   207→
   208→Apply annotations
   209→2. The Graph Research Labs 'base' ontology will import an annotation model called 'Annotation Properties Fragment', which constains the required annotations under the 'grl annotation properties' annotation property:
   210→- 'api description' - applied to an API resource, this annotation determines the full description shown for a given path in the generated YAML 
   211→- 'api summary' - applied to an API resource, this annotation determines the summary description shown for a given path in the generated YAML
   212→- 'api tag' -  - applied to an API resource, this annotation creates one or more tags used to organise and group paths in the generated YAML
   213→- 'api label' - applied to classes linked via the 'has resource schema', this annotation specifies what triples will be included in the resource schema attached to an API path, using the URI suffix of the API being generated as the value of the annotation e.g. as per the example above "EmploymentAPIV1.0.1". Nested schema can be created by applying this annotation to triples for subclassess. For example, adding this annotation/value to the triple 'has climate related capital deployment metric' exactly 1 'Capital Deployment Amount Metric' and its properties ensures they will all be included in the generated API schema.

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

content.content:
1→# CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this
     4→repository.
     5→
     6→## Project Overview
     7→
     8→ASG (API Specification Generator) generates OpenAPI 3.1 specification files (YAML/JSON) from OWL-RDF
     9→ontologies. It transforms semantic ontology structures into REST API specifications.
    10→
    11→## Build Commands
    12→
    13→```bash
    14→mvn clean package                    # Build and package
    15→mvn clean verify                     # Build with all tests (unit + integration)
    16→mvn clean package -DskipTests        # Build without tests
    17→mvn test                             # Run only unit tests
    18→mvn verify                           # Run integration tests
    19→mvn test -Dtest=NarrowTest           # Run single test class
    20→mvn test -P NoRegressionTests        # Build without regression tests (faster)
    21→```
    22→
    23→Output JARs in `target/`:
    24→
    25→- `asg-<version>-jar-with-dependencies.jar` - Executable JAR
    26→- `asg-<version>.jar` - Library JAR
    27→
    28→## Running the Tool
    29→
    30→```bash
    31→java -jar target/asg-*-jar-with-dependencies.jar -c <config-file>
    32→java -jar target/asg-*-jar-with-dependencies.jar -c <config-file> -G  # Stop after flattening (debug)
    33→```
    34→
    35→## Architecture
    36→
    37→### Pipeline Flow
    38→
    39→```
    40→Config Loading → Ontology Loading → Namespace Flattening → OWL→OpenAPI Mapping → Output
    41→```
    42→
    43→### Key Components
    44→
    45→**Entry Point:** `Asg.java` - CLI and orchestration
    46→
    47→**Core Mappers** (`com.grl.component.mapper/`):
    48→
    49→- `OntologyMapper` - Main orchestrator: loads ontology, generates OpenAPI
    50→- `ResourceSchemaMapper` - Maps OWL class to OpenAPI Schema
    51→- `DataPropertyMapper` / `ObjectPropertyMapper` / `TaxonomyPropertyMapper` - Property mapping
    52→  strategies
    53→
    54→**Schema Processing** (`com.grl.component.schema/`):
    55→
    56→- `SchemaVisitor` - OWL class expression visitor (Visitor pattern), collects restrictions
    57→- `RestrictionInfo` - Sealed interface with 16 record types for type-safe OWL restrictions
    58→- `PropertyNameResolver` - Resolves IRI short forms for property names
    59→
    60→**OWL Utilities** (`com.grl.component.owl/`):
    61→
    62→- `OntologyUtil` - OWL API helpers
    63→- `Narrow<T,R>` - Type-safe casting function for streams
    64→- `WithAnnotation` / `WithIRI` - Predicate factories for filtering
    65→
    66→**Configuration** (`com.grl.component.asg.configuration/`):
    67→
    68→- `Config` - Loads YAML with inheritance (`extends` field) and variable substitution
    69→- `YamlConfig` - Configuration POJO with Jakarta validation
    70→
    71→### Key Patterns
    72→
    73→1. **Visitor Pattern**: `SchemaVisitor` implements `OWLObjectVisitor` to traverse class hierarchies
    74→2. **Sealed Interface**: `RestrictionInfo` uses sealed types for compile-time exhaustiveness
    75→   checking
    76→3. **Context Object**: `MappingContext` maintains shared state with thread-safe schema cache
    77→
    78→## Testing
    79→
    80→**Unit tests:** `/src/test/java/com/grl/component/` - Match `**/*.java`
    81→
    82→**Integration tests:** `/src/test/java/regression/` - Match `**/*IT.java`
    83→
    84→- `SimpleApiIT` - Tests against employment ontology
    85→- `LargeApiIT` - Tests against business ontology (requires git submodule)
    86→
    87→**Test utilities:** `TestUtil.java` - Config loading, spec generation, caching helpers
    88→
    89→## Refactoring Guidelines
    90→
    91→1. **Leverage OWL API**: Use OWL API classes instead of custom implementations
    92→2. **Type Safety**: Utilize generics and typed collections
    93→3. **Immutability**: Prefer immutable OWL objects
    94→4. Ensure both unit and integration tests pass after changes
    95→
    96→## Output Structure
    97→
    98→```
    99→output/<api-name>/
   100→├── effective-config.yaml    # Merged config
   101→├── ontology0.owl           # Original ontology copy
   102→├── ontology-flat.owl       # Namespace-flattened
   103→├── ontology-merged.owl     # All imports merged
   104→├── openapi.json            # Generated spec (JSON)
   105→└── openapi.yaml            # Generated spec (YAML)
   106→```
   107→
   108→## Key Dependencies
   109→
   110→- **OWL API 5.1.20** - Ontology manipulation
   111→- **Swagger/OpenAPI 7.8.0** - Spec generation
   112→- **Jackson 2.18.0** - YAML/JSON serialization
   113→- **JUnit 5 / Mockito** - Testing
   114→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

data.prompt:
Design a comprehensive end-user documentation structure for ASG (API Specification Generator) - a tool that generates OpenAPI specs from OWL ontologies.

**Context from exploration:**
- Current README.md covers basics but is dense
- Tool has: CLI options (-c config, -f ontology, -o output, -G flatten-only), extensive YAML config with inheritance, namespace mappings
- Supports 16 OWL restriction types (cardinality, value restrictions, boolean combinations)
- Maps 46 XSD datatypes to OpenAPI types
- Uses annotations: apiLabel, apiDescription, apiSummary, apiTag, apiID, security, skos:example, skos:prefLabel
- Outputs: openapi.yaml/json, ontology files (original, merged, flat), effective-config.yaml

**Requirements:**
1. Create docs/ folder structure with separate files
2. Target end-users (how to use, not how it works internally)
3. Should enable exhaustive integration test creation

**Design:**
1. List all documentation files needed with their purposes
2. Outline content for each file (sections, topics)
3. Identify which documented features will need corresponding integration tests
4. Consider a feature matrix that maps OWL constructs → OpenAPI output

Provide a detailed documentation plan.

content.content:
1→name: "default_project"
     2→outputDir: "outputs"
     3→defaultSecurityTemplate: "{action}:{api}:{model}"
     4→securityTemplate: "{action}:{api}:{security}"
     5→securityActionMappings:
     6→  GET: read
     7→  HEAD: read
     8→  POST: write
     9→  PUT: write
    10→  PATCH: write
    11→  DELETE: write
    12→templates:
    13→  openapi:
    14→    openapi: 3.1.0
    15→    components:
    16→      responses:
    17→        "400":
    18→          description: Bad Request
    19→        "401":
    20→          description: Unauthorized
    21→        "403":
    22→          description: Forbidden
    23→        "404":
    24→          description: Not found.
    25→        "405":
    26→          description: Method Not Allowed
    27→        "406":
    28→          description: Not acceptable
    29→        "429":
    30→          description: Too Many Requests
    31→        "500":
    32→          description: Internal Server Error
    33→
    34→  collectionPathItem:
    35→    # Default responses merged in for API endpoints which handle collection listing/post
    36→    get:
    37→      description: "Fetch a list of items."
    38→      responses:
    39→        "400":
    40→          $ref: "#/components/responses/400"
    41→        "401":
    42→          $ref: "#/components/responses/401"
    43→        "403":
    44→          $ref: "#/components/responses/403"
    45→        "406":
    46→          $ref: "#/components/responses/406"
    47→        "429":
    48→          $ref: "#/components/responses/429"
    49→        "500":
    50→          $ref: "#/components/responses/500"
    51→    post:
    52→      description: "Create a new item. NOTE: Any unique identifier in the request body will be ignored"
    53→      responses:
    54→        "400":
    55→          $ref: "#/components/responses/400"
    56→        "401":
    57→          $ref: "#/components/responses/401"
    58→        "403":
    59→          $ref: "#/components/responses/403"
    60→        "406":
    61→          $ref: "#/components/responses/406"
    62→        "429":
    63→          $ref: "#/components/responses/429"
    64→        "500":
    65→          $ref: "#/components/responses/500"
    66→  singlePathItem:
    67→    # Default responses merged in for API endpoints which handle collection listing/post
    68→    get:
    69→      description: "Fetch an item given a known id"
    70→      responses:
    71→        "401":
    72→          $ref: "#/components/responses/401"
    73→        "403":
    74→          $ref: "#/components/responses/403"
    75→        "404":
    76→          $ref: "#/components/responses/404"
    77→        "406":
    78→          $ref: "#/components/responses/406"
    79→        "429":
    80→          $ref: "#/components/responses/429"
    81→        "500":
    82→          $ref: "#/components/responses/500"
    83→    delete:
    84→      description: "Remove an item given a known id"
    85→      responses:
    86→        "401":
    87→          $ref: "#/components/responses/401"
    88→        "403":
    89→          $ref: "#/components/responses/403"
    90→        "404":
    91→          $ref: "#/components/responses/404"
    92→        "406":
    93→          $ref: "#/components/responses/406"
    94→        "429":
    95→          $ref: "#/components/responses/429"
    96→        "500":
    97→          $ref: "#/components/responses/500"
    98→    put:
    99→      description: "Update an item given a known id. NOTE: the unique identifier will not be updated by this operation"
   100→      responses:
   101→        "400":
   102→          $ref: "#/components/responses/400"
   103→        "401":
   104→          $ref: "#/components/responses/401"
   105→        "403":
   106→          $ref: "#/components/responses/403"
   107→        "404":
   108→          $ref: "#/components/responses/404"
   109→        "406":
   110→          $ref: "#/components/responses/406"
   111→        "429":
   112→          $ref: "#/components/responses/429"
   113→        "500":
   114→          $ref: "#/components/responses/500"
   115→    patch:
   116→      description: "Selectively update fields of an item given a known id. NOTE: the unique identifier will not be updated by this operation"
   117→      responses:
   118→        "400":
   119→          $ref: "#/components/responses/400"
   120→        "401":
   121→          $ref: "#/components/responses/401"
   122→        "403":
   123→          $ref: "#/components/responses/403"
   124→        "404":
   125→          $ref: "#/components/responses/404"

Design an exhaustive integration test suite for ASG (API Specification Generator) using purpose-built test ontologies.

**Context from exploration:**
- Current tests: SimpleApiIT (10 tests, employment ontology), LargeApiIT (14 tests, NZCS ontology)
- Test pattern: Load config → Run Asg.main() → Parse JSON with JsonPath → Assert paths/values
- TestUtil provides caching for generated specs

**Tool capabilities to test:**
1. OWL Restriction Types (16 types):
   - Object cardinality: MinCardinality, MaxCardinality, ExactCardinality
   - Data cardinality: DataMinCardinality, DataMaxCardinality, DataExactCardinality
   - Value restrictions: SomeValuesFrom, AllValuesFrom, HasValue, OneOf
   - Data value: DataSomeValuesFrom, DataAllValuesFrom, DataHasValue, DataOneOf
   - Boolean: UnionOf, IntersectionOf, ComplementOf

2. XSD Datatype Mappings (46 types):
   - String types: string, anyURI, base64Binary, hexBinary, etc.
   - Numeric: integer, long, int, short, decimal, float, double
   - Boolean, temporal (date, dateTime, time, duration)

3. Property Types:
   - Data properties → primitive schemas
   - Object properties → $ref schemas, arrays, x-grl-model-link
   - Taxonomy properties → enum schemas

- Supports 16 OWL restriction types (cardinality, value restrictions, boolean combinations)
- Maps 46 XSD datatypes to OpenAPI types
- Uses annotations: apiLabel, apiDescription, apiSummary, apiTag, apiID, security, skos:example, skos:prefLabel
- Outputs: openapi.yaml/json, ontology files (original, merged, flat), effective-config.yaml

**Requirements:**
1. Create docs/ folder structure with separate files
2. Target end-users (how to use, not how it works internally)
3. Should enable exhaustive integration test creation

**Design:**
1. List all documentation files needed with their purposes
2. Outline content for each file (sections, topics)
3. Identify which documented features will need corresponding integration tests
4. Consider a feature matrix that maps OWL constructs → OpenAPI output

data.prompt:
Design an exhaustive integration test suite for ASG (API Specification Generator) using purpose-built test ontologies.

**Context from exploration:**
- Current tests: SimpleApiIT (10 tests, employment ontology), LargeApiIT (14 tests, NZCS ontology)
- Test pattern: Load config → Run Asg.main() → Parse JSON with JsonPath → Assert paths/values
- TestUtil provides caching for generated specs

**Tool capabilities to test:**
1. OWL Restriction Types (16 types):
   - Object cardinality: MinCardinality, MaxCardinality, ExactCardinality
   - Data cardinality: DataMinCardinality, DataMaxCardinality, DataExactCardinality
   - Value restrictions: SomeValuesFrom, AllValuesFrom, HasValue, OneOf
   - Data value: DataSomeValuesFrom, DataAllValuesFrom, DataHasValue, DataOneOf
   - Boolean: UnionOf, IntersectionOf, ComplementOf

2. XSD Datatype Mappings (46 types):
   - String types: string, anyURI, base64Binary, hexBinary, etc.
   - Numeric: integer, long, int, short, decimal, float, double
   - Boolean, temporal (date, dateTime, time, duration)

3. Property Types:
   - Data properties → primitive schemas
   - Object properties → $ref schemas, arrays, x-grl-model-link
   - Taxonomy properties → enum schemas

4. Configuration Options:
   - extends (config inheritance)
   - rootClass (auto-detect vs explicit)
   - namespaceMappings (flattening)
   - enforceRequiredness
   - security templates and mappings
   - templates (openapi, collectionPathItem, singlePathItem)

5. Annotations:
   - apiLabel, apiDescription, apiSummary, apiTag, apiID, security
   - skos:example, skos:prefLabel, skos:definition

6. Output Validation:
   - Path generation (collection/single endpoints)
   - Schema types and compositions
   - Security requirements
   - Examples

**Requirements:**
1. Create new purpose-built test ontologies (small, focused)
2. Prioritize feature coverage (systematic testing of all constructs)
3. Organize tests logically

**Design:**
1. Propose test organization (by feature area? by OWL construct?)
2. List test ontologies needed with their purposes
3. Map features to specific test cases
4. Provide naming conventions and file structure

Design a comprehensive end-user documentation structure for ASG (API Specification Generator) - a tool that generates OpenAPI specs from OWL ontologies.

**Context from exploration:**
- Current README.md covers basics but is dense
- Tool has: CLI options (-c config, -f ontology, -o output, -G flatten-only), extensive YAML config with inheritance, namespace mappings
- Supports 16 OWL restriction types (cardinality, value restrictions, boolean combinations)
- Maps 46 XSD datatypes to OpenAPI types
- Uses annotations: apiLabel, apiDescription, apiSummary, apiTag, apiID, security, skos:example, skos:prefLabel
- Outputs: openapi.yaml/json, ontology files (original, merged, flat), effective-config.yaml

**Requirements:**
1. Create docs/ folder structure with separate files
2. Target end-users (how to use, not how it works internally)
3. Should enable exhaustive integration test creation

**Design:**
1. List all documentation files needed with their purposes
2. Outline content for each file (sections, topics)
3. Identify which documented features will need corresponding integration tests
4. Consider a feature matrix that maps OWL constructs → OpenAPI output

Provide a detailed documentation plan.

     4→     xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"

     9→     xmlns:gist="https://w3id.org/semanticarts/ns/ontology/gist/"
    10→     xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
    11→     xmlns:skos="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#"
    12→     xmlns:shacl="http://www.w3.org/ns/shacl#"
    13→     xmlns:terms="http://purl.org/dc/terms/"
    14→     xmlns:GRLAnnotations="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/GRLAnnotations/">
    15→    <owl:Ontology rdf:about="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/employment/">
    16→        <owl:versionIRI rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/employment/v1.0.1"/>
    17→        <owl:imports rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/V1.0.2"/>
    18→        <rdfs:comment>Test ontology, used for unit testing.</rdfs:comment>
    19→        <owl:versionInfo>1.0.1</owl:versionInfo>
    20→        <owl:versionInfo>Copyright Graph Research Labs Limited, 2025.</owl:versionInfo>
    21→    </owl:Ontology>
    22→    
    23→
    24→
    25→    <!-- 
    26→    ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
    27→    //
    28→    // Data properties
    29→    //
    30→    ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
    31→     -->
    32→
    33→

    35→

    37→
    38→    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/apiID">
    39→        <rdfs:subPropertyOf rdf:resource="https://w3id.org/semanticarts/ns/ontology/gist/exponentOfNumber"/>
    40→    </rdf:Description>
    41→    
    42→
    43→
    44→    <!-- 
    45→    ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
    46→    //
    47→    // Classes
    48→    //
    49→    ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
    50→     -->
    51→
    52→    
    53→
    54→

    56→
    57→    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/APIRoot">
    58→        <rdfs:subClassOf>
    59→            <owl:Restriction>
    60→                <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/hasAPI"/>
    61→                <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/employment/EmploymentAPIV1.0.1"/>
    62→            </owl:Restriction>
    63→        </rdfs:subClassOf>
    64→    </rdf:Description>
    65→    
    66→
    67→

data.prompt:
Design an exhaustive integration test suite for ASG (API Specification Generator) using purpose-built test ontologies.

**Context from exploration:**
- Current tests: SimpleApiIT (10 tests, employment ontology), LargeApiIT (14 tests, NZCS ontology)
- Test pattern: Load config → Run Asg.main() → Parse JSON with JsonPath → Assert paths/values
- TestUtil provides caching for generated specs

**Tool capabilities to test:**
1. OWL Restriction Types (16 types):
   - Object cardinality: MinCardinality, MaxCardinality, ExactCardinality
   - Data cardinality: DataMinCardinality, DataMaxCardinality, DataExactCardinality
   - Value restrictions: SomeValuesFrom, AllValuesFrom, HasValue, OneOf
   - Data value: DataSomeValuesFrom, DataAllValuesFrom, DataHasValue, DataOneOf
   - Boolean: UnionOf, IntersectionOf, ComplementOf

2. XSD Datatype Mappings (46 types):
   - String types: string, anyURI, base64Binary, hexBinary, etc.
   - Numeric: integer, long, int, short, decimal, float, double
   - Boolean, temporal (date, dateTime, time, duration)

3. Property Types:
   - Data properties → primitive schemas
   - Object properties → $ref schemas, arrays, x-grl-model-link
   - Taxonomy properties → enum schemas

4. Configuration Options:
   - extends (config inheritance)
   - rootClass (auto-detect vs explicit)
   - namespaceMappings (flattening)
   - enforceRequiredness
   - security templates and mappings
   - templates (openapi, collectionPathItem, singlePathItem)

5. Annotations:
   - apiLabel, apiDescription, apiSummary, apiTag, apiID, security
   - skos:example, skos:prefLabel, skos:definition

6. Output Validation:
   - Path generation (collection/single endpoints)
   - Schema types and compositions
   - Security requirements
   - Examples

**Requirements:**
1. Create new purpose-built test ontologies (small, focused)
2. Prioritize feature coverage (systematic testing of all constructs)
3. Organize tests logically

**Design:**
1. Propose test organization (by feature area? by OWL construct?)
2. List test ontologies needed with their purposes
3. Map features to specific test cases
4. Provide naming conventions and file structure

Design a comprehensive end-user documentation structure for ASG (API Specification Generator) - a tool that generates OpenAPI specs from OWL ontologies.

**Context from exploration:**
- Current README.md covers basics but is dense
- Tool has: CLI options (-c config, -f ontology, -o output, -G flatten-only), extensive YAML config with inheritance, namespace mappings
- Supports 16 OWL restriction types (cardinality, value restrictions, boolean combinations)
- Maps 46 XSD datatypes to OpenAPI types
- Uses annotations: apiLabel, apiDescription, apiSummary, apiTag, apiID, security, skos:example, skos:prefLabel
- Outputs: openapi.yaml/json, ontology files (original, merged, flat), effective-config.yaml

**Requirements:**
1. Create docs/ folder structure with separate files
2. Target end-users (how to use, not how it works internally)
3. Should enable exhaustive integration test creation

**Design:**
1. List all documentation files needed with their purposes
2. Outline content for each file (sections, topics)
3. Identify which documented features will need corresponding integration tests
4. Consider a feature matrix that maps OWL constructs → OpenAPI output

Provide a detailed documentation plan.

Design an exhaustive integration test suite for ASG (API Specification Generator) using purpose-built test ontologies.

**Context from exploration:**
- Current tests: SimpleApiIT (10 tests, employment ontology), LargeApiIT (14 tests, NZCS ontology)
- Test pattern: Load config → Run Asg.main() → Parse JSON with JsonPath → Assert paths/values
- TestUtil provides caching for generated specs

**Tool capabilities to test:**
1. OWL Restriction Types (16 types):
   - Object cardinality: MinCardinality, MaxCardinality, ExactCardinality
   - Data cardinality: DataMinCardinality, DataMaxCardinality, DataExactCardinality
   - Value restrictions: SomeValuesFrom, AllValuesFrom, HasValue, OneOf
   - Data value: DataSomeValuesFrom, DataAllValuesFrom, DataHasValue, DataOneOf
   - Boolean: UnionOf, IntersectionOf, ComplementOf

2. XSD Datatype Mappings (46 types):
   - String types: string, anyURI, base64Binary, hexBinary, etc.
   - Numeric: integer, long, int, short, decimal, float, double
   - Boolean, temporal (date, dateTime, time, duration)

3. Property Types:
   - Data properties → primitive schemas
   - Object properties → $ref schemas, arrays, x-grl-model-link
   - Taxonomy properties → enum schemas

4. Configuration Options:
   - extends (config inheritance)
   - rootClass (auto-detect vs explicit)
   - namespaceMappings (flattening)
   - enforceRequiredness
   - security templates and mappings
   - templates (openapi, collectionPathItem, singlePathItem)

5. Annotations:
   - apiLabel, apiDescription, apiSummary, apiTag, apiID, security
   - skos:example, skos:prefLabel, skos:definition

6. Output Validation:
   - Path generation (collection/single endpoints)
   - Schema types and compositions
   - Security requirements
   - Examples

**Requirements:**
1. Create new purpose-built test ontologies (small, focused)
2. Prioritize feature coverage (systematic testing of all constructs)
3. Organize tests logically

**Design:**
1. Propose test organization (by feature area? by OWL construct?)
2. List test ontologies needed with their purposes
3. Map features to specific test cases
4. Provide naming conventions and file structure

    10→  DELETE: delete

    18→      title: NZ Climate Disclosure Standard 1 API
    19→      version: 1.0.1

    42→              tokenUrl: https://dev-3kuyljqso8qiqmqd.us.auth0.com/oauth/token
    43→              x-grl-audience: https://graph.climatetracker.com/nzcs1
    44→              scopes:

    57→        description: description

Design a comprehensive end-user documentation structure for ASG (API Specification Generator) - a tool that generates OpenAPI specs from OWL ontologies.

**Context from exploration:**
- Current README.md covers basics but is dense
- Tool has: CLI options (-c config, -f ontology, -o output, -G flatten-only), extensive YAML config with inheritance, namespace mappings
- Supports 16 OWL restriction types (cardinality, value restrictions, boolean combinations)
- Maps 46 XSD datatypes to OpenAPI types
- Uses annotations: apiLabel, apiDescription, apiSummary, apiTag, apiID, security, skos:example, skos:prefLabel
- Outputs: openapi.yaml/json, ontology files (original, merged, flat), effective-config.yaml

**Requirements:**
1. Create docs/ folder structure with separate files
2. Target end-users (how to use, not how it works internally)
3. Should enable exhaustive integration test creation

**Design:**
1. List all documentation files needed with their purposes
2. Outline content for each file (sections, topics)
3. Identify which documented features will need corresponding integration tests
4. Consider a feature matrix that maps OWL constructs → OpenAPI output

Provide a detailed documentation plan.

data.prompt:
Design a comprehensive end-user documentation structure for ASG (API Specification Generator) - a tool that generates OpenAPI specs from OWL ontologies.

**Context from exploration:**
- Current README.md covers basics but is dense
- Tool has: CLI options (-c config, -f ontology, -o output, -G flatten-only), extensive YAML config with inheritance, namespace mappings
- Supports 16 OWL restriction types (cardinality, value restrictions, boolean combinations)
- Maps 46 XSD datatypes to OpenAPI types
- Uses annotations: apiLabel, apiDescription, apiSummary, apiTag, apiID, security, skos:example, skos:prefLabel
- Outputs: openapi.yaml/json, ontology files (original, merged, flat), effective-config.yaml

**Requirements:**
1. Create docs/ folder structure with separate files
2. Target end-users (how to use, not how it works internally)
3. Should enable exhaustive integration test creation

**Design:**
1. List all documentation files needed with their purposes
2. Outline content for each file (sections, topics)
3. Identify which documented features will need corresponding integration tests
4. Consider a feature matrix that maps OWL constructs → OpenAPI output

Provide a detailed documentation plan.

Design an exhaustive integration test suite for ASG (API Specification Generator) using purpose-built test ontologies.

**Context from exploration:**
- Current tests: SimpleApiIT (10 tests, employment ontology), LargeApiIT (14 tests, NZCS ontology)
- Test pattern: Load config → Run Asg.main() → Parse JSON with JsonPath → Assert paths/values
- TestUtil provides caching for generated specs

**Tool capabilities to test:**
1. OWL Restriction Types (16 types):
   - Object cardinality: MinCardinality, MaxCardinality, ExactCardinality
   - Data cardinality: DataMinCardinality, DataMaxCardinality, DataExactCardinality
   - Value restrictions: SomeValuesFrom, AllValuesFrom, HasValue, OneOf
   - Data value: DataSomeValuesFrom, DataAllValuesFrom, DataHasValue, DataOneOf
   - Boolean: UnionOf, IntersectionOf, ComplementOf

2. XSD Datatype Mappings (46 types):
   - String types: string, anyURI, base64Binary, hexBinary, etc.
   - Numeric: integer, long, int, short, decimal, float, double
   - Boolean, temporal (date, dateTime, time, duration)

3. Property Types:
   - Data properties → primitive schemas
   - Object properties → $ref schemas, arrays, x-grl-model-link
   - Taxonomy properties → enum schemas

4. Configuration Options:
   - extends (config inheritance)
   - rootClass (auto-detect vs explicit)
   - namespaceMappings (flattening)
   - enforceRequiredness
   - security templates and mappings
   - templates (openapi, collectionPathItem, singlePathItem)

5. Annotations:
   - apiLabel, apiDescription, apiSummary, apiTag, apiID, security
   - skos:example, skos:prefLabel, skos:definition

6. Output Validation:
   - Path generation (collection/single endpoints)
   - Schema types and compositions
   - Security requirements
   - Examples

**Requirements:**
1. Create new purpose-built test ontologies (small, focused)
2. Prioritize feature coverage (systematic testing of all constructs)
3. Organize tests logically

**Design:**
1. Propose test organization (by feature area? by OWL construct?)
2. List test ontologies needed with their purposes
3. Map features to specific test cases
4. Provide naming conventions and file structure

content.text:
I now have a comprehensive understanding of the ASG tool. Let me provide the detailed documentation plan.

---

## Comprehensive Documentation Plan for ASG (API Specification Generator)

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, I have identified all features, configuration options, OWL restriction types, datatype mappings, annotations, and output formats. Below is a detailed documentation structure designed for end-users.

---

### 1. Documentation Files Structure

```
docs/
├── index.md                           # Landing page / overview
├── getting-started.md                 # Quick start guide
├── installation.md                    # Prerequisites and installation
├── cli-reference.md                   # Command-line interface reference
├── configuration/
│   ├── index.md                       # Configuration overview
│   ├── config-reference.md            # Complete YAML config reference
│   ├── config-inheritance.md          # extends and variable substitution
│   ├── templates.md                   # OpenAPI templates customization
│   └── security.md                    # Security configuration
├── ontology-design/
│   ├── index.md                       # Ontology requirements overview
│   ├── structure.md                   # Required ontology structure
│   ├── annotations.md                 # All supported annotations
│   ├── restrictions.md                # OWL restrictions and their mappings
│   └── datatypes.md                   # XSD datatype mappings
├── mapping-reference/
│   ├── index.md                       # Mapping overview
│   ├── owl-to-openapi-matrix.md       # Feature matrix: OWL → OpenAPI
│   ├── data-properties.md             # Data property mapping details
│   ├── object-properties.md           # Object property mapping details
│   └── taxonomy-properties.md         # Taxonomy/enum mapping details
├── output/
│   ├── index.md                       # Output files overview
│   ├── openapi-structure.md           # Generated OpenAPI structure
│   └── extensions.md                  # Custom x-grl-* extensions
├── examples/
│   ├── minimal-example.md             # Minimal working example
│   ├── full-example.md                # Complete real-world example
│   └── common-patterns.md             # Common OWL patterns and their output
├── troubleshooting.md                 # Common issues and solutions
└── glossary.md                        # OWL/OpenAPI terminology
```

---

### 2. Detailed Content Outline for Each File

#### **docs/index.md** - Landing Page
**Purpose**: High-level overview and navigation

**Content**:
- What is ASG
- Key capabilities summary
- When to use ASG
- Quick navigation to main sections
- Version compatibility (OWL API 5.1.20, OpenAPI 3.1)

---

#### **docs/getting-started.md** - Quick Start Guide
**Purpose**: Get users running in 5 minutes

**Content**:
1. Minimal prerequisites (Java 21+)
2. Download/build instructions (one command)
3. Simplest possible config file example
4. Run command
5. Expected output explanation
6. Link to full examples

---

#### **docs/installation.md** - Installation
**Purpose**: Complete setup instructions

**Content**:
- **Prerequisites**:
  - Java 21+
  - Maven 3.6.3+ (if building)
  - OWL ontology file
  - Optional: XML catalog file

- **Installation Methods**:
  - Download pre-built JAR
  - Build from source (`mvn clean package`)
  - Maven dependency integration
  - Maven plugin integration (exec-maven-plugin example)

- **JAR Files Produced**:
  - `asg-<version>-jar-with-dependencies.jar` - Standalone executable
  - `asg-<version>.jar` - Library JAR

---

#### **docs/cli-reference.md** - CLI Reference
**Purpose**: Complete command-line documentation

**Content**:
| Option | Long Form | Required | Description |
|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `-c` | `--config` | Yes | Configuration file path |
| `-f` | `--ontology-file` | No | Override ontology from config |
| `-o` | `--output-dir` | No | Override output directory from config |
| `-G` | `--no-generate` | No | Stop after flattening ontology (debug mode) |

**Examples**:
```bash
# Basic usage
java -jar asg-*-jar-with-dependencies.jar -c config.yaml

# Override ontology
java -jar asg-*-jar-with-dependencies.jar -c config.yaml -f my-ontology.owl

# Debug mode - only flatten
java -jar asg-*-jar-with-dependencies.jar -c config.yaml -G
```

---

#### **docs/configuration/index.md** - Configuration Overview
**Purpose**: Configuration system introduction

**Content**:
- Configuration file format (YAML)
- Inheritance mechanism overview
- Variable substitution (`${configDir}`)
- Default values (from `default.config.yaml`)
- Path resolution rules (relative to config file location)

---

#### **docs/configuration/config-reference.md** - Complete Config Reference
**Purpose**: Exhaustive reference of all config options

**Content** (organized by section):

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `name` | string | Yes | `default_project` | API identifier (alphanumeric + underscore only) |
| `ontologies` | list[URI] | Yes | - | List of OWL ontology files/URIs |
| `outputDir` | path | Yes | `outputs` | Output directory for generated files |
| `rootClass` | IRI | No | Auto-detect | IRI of root API class |
| `catalogFile` | path | No | - | XML catalog for ontology imports |
| `enforceRequiredness` | boolean | No | false | Add `required` based on cardinality |
| `extends` | path | No | - | Parent config to inherit from |
| `namespaceMappings` | map | No | - | Namespace flattening rules |
| `securitySchemeName` | string | No | Auto-detect | OAuth2 security scheme name |
| `defaultSecurityTemplate` | string | No | `{action}:{api}:{model}` | Security scope pattern (no annotation) |
| `securityTemplate` | string | No | `{action}:{api}:{security}` | Security scope pattern (with annotation) |
| `securityActionMappings` | map | No | See defaults | HTTP method to action mapping |
| `templates.openapi` | OpenAPI | No | - | Base OpenAPI template |
| `templates.collectionPathItem` | PathItem | No | - | Template for collection endpoints |
| `templates.singlePathItem` | PathItem | No | - | Template for single-item endpoints |

**Default Security Action Mappings**:
```yaml
securityActionMappings:
  GET: read
  HEAD: read
  POST: write
  PUT: write
  PATCH: write
  DELETE: write
```

---

#### **docs/configuration/config-inheritance.md** - Config Inheritance
**Purpose**: Explain extends mechanism and variable substitution

**Content**:
- **`extends` field**: How configs inherit from parent configs
- **Merge behavior**: Lists concatenate, maps merge (child wins)
- **Variable substitution**: `${configDir}` resolves to config file directory
- **Resolution order**: Child → Parent → default.config.yaml
- **Example**: Multi-environment setup with base config

---

#### **docs/configuration/templates.md** - OpenAPI Templates
**Purpose**: Customizing generated OpenAPI output

**Content**:
- **templates.openapi**: Base OpenAPI document structure
  - info, servers, components, externalDocs
  - How generated content merges

- **templates.collectionPathItem**: Template for `GET /Resource`, `POST /Resource`
  - Default responses (400, 401, 403, 406, 429, 500)

- **templates.singlePathItem**: Template for `GET/PUT/PATCH/DELETE /Resource/{id}`
  - Default responses (401, 403, 404, 406, 429, 500)

- **Merge rules**: Template provides defaults, generated content overlays

---

#### **docs/configuration/security.md** - Security Configuration
**Purpose**: OAuth2 and API key security configuration

**Content**:
- **Security scheme types**: OAuth2, API Key
- **Security templates**: Placeholders `{api}`, `{action}`, `{model}`, `{security}`
- **Per-resource security**: Using `security` annotation on `hasResourceSchema`
- **Scope generation**: How scopes are derived from templates
- **OAuth2 scope RFC compliance**: RFC 6749 section 3.3

---

#### **docs/ontology-design/index.md** - Ontology Requirements Overview
**Purpose**: Summary of what makes an ontology "ASG-ready"

**Content**:
- Required base ontology import (GRL base)
- API Root class requirement
- hasResourceSchema and hasAPI properties
- Annotation properties fragment
- apiLabel filtering mechanism

---

#### **docs/ontology-design/structure.md** - Required Ontology Structure
**Purpose**: Detailed structural requirements

**Content**:
- **API Root Class**: Entry point linking to API specification
- **Specification Entry subclass**: Named with version (e.g., `EmploymentAPIV1.0.1`)
- **hasAPI property**: Links API Root to specification class
- **hasResourceSchema property**: Defines resources to generate
  - Exactly/min/max cardinality patterns
  - Multiple resources from single root
- **apiID property**: Required on each resource class for REST identity

**Diagram**:
```
APIRoot
    └── subClassOf: hasAPI some EmploymentAPIV1.0.1
                    └── subClassOf: hasResourceSchema exactly 1 Employee
                                    hasResourceSchema exactly 1 Employer
```

---

#### **docs/ontology-design/annotations.md** - Supported Annotations
**Purpose**: Complete list of all annotation properties recognized

**Content**:

| Annotation | IRI Prefix | Purpose | Applied To |
|------------|-----------|---------|------------|
| `apiLabel` | GRL | Filter axioms for this API version | SubClassOf axioms |
| `apiDescription` | GRL | Full description for paths | Resource classes |
| `apiSummary` | GRL | Short summary for paths | Resource classes |
| `apiTag` | GRL | Grouping tags | Resource classes |
| `apiDisplayLabel` | GRL | Human-readable title | Classes/properties |
| `apiID` | GRL | Marks class as having REST identity | Resource classes |
| `security` | GRL | Security groups for resource | hasResourceSchema axioms |
| `skos:prefLabel` | SKOS | Preferred display label | Classes/properties |
| `skos:definition` | SKOS | Description fallback | Classes/properties |
| `skos:example` | SKOS | Example values for properties | Data properties, axioms |
| `rdfs:comment` | RDFS | Description fallback | Classes/properties |
| `prov:definition` | PROV | Description fallback | Classes/properties |

**Priority order for descriptions**: apiDescription > skos:definition > rdfs:comment > prov:definition

**Priority order for titles**: apiDisplayLabel > skos:prefLabel > IRI short form

---

#### **docs/ontology-design/restrictions.md** - OWL Restrictions Reference
**Purpose**: All 17 supported restriction types and their OpenAPI mapping

**Content**:

##### Object Property Restrictions

| OWL Restriction | OpenAPI Mapping | Example |
|-----------------|-----------------|---------|
| `ObjectSomeValuesFrom` | Array with `minItems: 1` | `hasAddress some Address` → array, required |
| `ObjectAllValuesFrom` | Type constraint only | `hasAddress only Address` → typed, not required |
| `ObjectMinCardinality(n)` | `minItems: n`, required if n>=1 | `hasPhone min 1` → minItems=1, required |
| `ObjectMaxCardinality(n)` | `maxItems: n` | `hasPhone max 3` → maxItems=3 |
| `ObjectExactCardinality(n)` | `minItems: n`, `maxItems: n` | `hasPhone exactly 2` → both set |
| `ObjectHasValue` | Reference to specific individual | `status value Active` |
| `ObjectOneOf` | Enum from named individuals | `oneOf(Active, Inactive)` → enum |
| `ObjectUnionOf` | `anyOf` composed schema | `Address or Location` → anyOf |
| `ObjectIntersectionOf` | `allOf` composed schema | `Person and Employee` → allOf |
| `ObjectComplementOf` | `not` schema | `not Manager` → not |

##### Data Property Restrictions

| OWL Restriction | OpenAPI Mapping | Example |
|-----------------|-----------------|---------|
| `DataSomeValuesFrom` | Array with `minItems: 1` | `hasEmail some string` → array |
| `DataAllValuesFrom` | Type constraint only | `hasEmail only string` → typed |
| `DataMinCardinality(n)` | `minItems: n` | `hasTag min 1` → minItems=1 |
| `DataMaxCardinality(n)` | `maxItems: n` | `hasTag max 5` → maxItems=5 |
| `DataExactCardinality(n)` | Both min/max | `hasCode exactly 1` → scalar |
| `DataHasValue` | `default` value | `status value "active"` → default |
| `DataOneOf` | `enum` values | `oneOf("A", "B", "C")` → enum |

##### Cardinality and Array Rules

- Cardinality = 1: Scalar property
- Cardinality > 1 or `someValuesFrom`: Array property
- `minCardinality >= 1` or `someValuesFrom`: Required property (when `enforceRequiredness: true`)

---

#### **docs/ontology-design/datatypes.md** - XSD Datatype Mappings
**Purpose**: Complete XSD to OpenAPI type mapping reference

**Content**:

| XSD Datatype | OpenAPI Type | OpenAPI Format | Notes |
|--------------|--------------|----------------|-------|
| `string` | string | - | Default |
| `boolean` | boolean | - | |
| `integer` | integer | - | |
| `int` | integer | - | |
| `long` | integer | int64 | |
| `short` | integer | - | |
| `byte` | integer | - | |
| `decimal` | number | - | |
| `float` | number | double | |
| `double` | number | double | |
| `dateTime` | string | date-time | ISO 8601 |
| `dateTimeStamp` | string | date-time | |
| `date` | string | - | |
| `time` | string | - | |
| `duration` | string | - | ISO 8601 duration |
| `anyURI` | string | uri | |
| `base64Binary` | string | byte | |
| `hexBinary` | string | - | |
| `language` | string | - | |
| `token` | string | - | |
| `normalizedString` | string | - | |
| `NMTOKEN` | string | - | |
| `Name` | string | - | |
| `NCName` | string | - | |
| `ID` | string | - | |
| `IDREF` | string | - | |
| `IDREFS` | string | - | |
| `ENTITY` | string | - | |
| `ENTITIES` | string | - | |
| `NOTATION` | string | - | |
| `QName` | string | - | |
| `anySimpleType` | string | - | |
| `anyType` | string | - | |
| `Literal` | string | - | RDF Literal |
| `langString` | string | - | RDF langString |
| `gDay` | string | - | |
| `gMonth` | string | - | |
| `gYear` | string | - | |
| `gMonthYear` | string | - | |
| `gYearMonth` | string | - | |
| `unsignedByte` | integer | - | |
| `unsignedShort` | integer | - | |
| `unsignedInt` | integer | - | |
| `unsignedLong` | integer | - | |
| `positiveInteger` | integer | - | |
| `negativeInteger` | integer | - | |
| `nonPositiveInteger` | integer | - | |
| `nonNegativeInteger` | integer | - | |

**Total**: 46 XSD datatypes supported

---

#### **docs/mapping-reference/owl-to-openapi-matrix.md** - Feature Matrix
**Purpose**: Quick reference matrix for OWL → OpenAPI transformation

**Content**:

##### Classes to Schemas

| OWL Construct | OpenAPI Output |
|---------------|----------------|
| OWL Class with apiLabel | `components/schemas/{ClassName}` |
| Class hierarchy (subclass with apiLabel) | Nested schema properties |
| Class annotations | Schema title, description |

##### Properties to Schema Properties

| OWL Construct | OpenAPI Output |
|---------------|----------------|
| DataProperty with restriction | Schema property (string/integer/etc) |
| ObjectProperty with restriction | Schema property ($ref or inline) |
| ObjectProperty to resource class | String with `x-grl-model-link` extension |
| ObjectProperty to non-resource | Inline object schema or $ref |

##### Resources to Paths

| OWL Construct | OpenAPI Output |
|---------------|----------------|
| hasResourceSchema restriction | Two paths: `/{Resource}` and `/{Resource}/{id}` |
| Resource with apiID | Path parameter `{ResourceId}` |
| Resource annotations | Path summary, description, tags |

##### HTTP Methods Generated

| Path Pattern | Methods |
|--------------|---------|
| `/{Resource}` | GET (list), POST (create) |
| `/{Resource}/{id}` | GET (read), PUT (replace), PATCH (update), DELETE (remove) |

#### **docs/mapping-reference/data-properties.md** - Data Property Mapping
**Purpose**: Detailed data property transformation rules

**Content**:

- Array vs scalar determination

  - From `skos:example` on property

- Enum generation from `DataOneOf`
- Default values from `DataHasValue`
- Union/Intersection handling (anyOf/allOf)

#### **docs/mapping-reference/object-properties.md** - Object Property Mapping
**Purpose**: Detailed object property transformation rules

**Content**:
- Reference vs inline schema decision
- Resource linking (`x-grl-model-link` extension)
- Array schemas for multi-valued properties
- Cardinality constraint application
- Range class schema generation
- Recursive schema handling
- ComposedSchema for multiple ranges

---

#### **docs/mapping-reference/taxonomy-properties.md** - Taxonomy Property Mapping
**Purpose**: Enum/taxonomy generation from named individuals

**Content**:
- Detection: ObjectProperty with named individuals in restriction
- Two patterns:
  1. Direct individuals: `status some oneOf(Active, Inactive)`
  2. Class-based: `status some StatusType` where StatusType has individuals
- Label resolution for enum values
- Array taxonomies

#### **docs/output/index.md** - Output Files Overview
**Purpose**: Explain all generated files

**Content**:

```
output/{api-name}/
├── effective-config.yaml    # Merged configuration (debug)
├── ontology0.owl            # Copy of input ontology
├── ontology-merged.owl      # All imports merged
├── ontology-flat.owl        # Namespace-flattened
├── openapi.json             # Generated spec (JSON)
└── openapi.yaml             # Generated spec (YAML)
```

| File | Purpose | When Useful |
|------|---------|-------------|
| effective-config.yaml | Shows resolved config after inheritance | Debugging config issues |
| ontology0.owl | Original input | Comparison |
| ontology-merged.owl | All imports resolved | Understanding full ontology |
| ontology-flat.owl | Single namespace | Debugging namespace issues |
| openapi.yaml/json | Final API specification | The output you want |

---

#### **docs/output/extensions.md** - Custom Extensions
**Purpose**: Document GRL-specific OpenAPI extensions

**Content**:

| Extension | Location | Value | Purpose |
|-----------|----------|-------|---------|
| `x-grl-api-label` | info | API version label | Links spec to ontology version |
| `x-grl-graph-namespace` | info | Target namespace IRI | Links to flattened ontology namespace |
| `x-grl-model-link` | string schema | Resource name | Indicates ID references another resource |

**Example**:
```yaml
info:
  x-grl-api-label: "EmploymentAPIV1.0.1"
  x-grl-graph-namespace: "https://example.com/employment/"

components:
  schemas:
    Employee:
      properties:
        employerId:
          type: string
          x-grl-model-link: "Employer"
```

#### **docs/examples/common-patterns.md** - Common Patterns
**Purpose**: Recipe-style solutions for common scenarios

**Content**:
- Required property: Use `exactly 1` or `min 1`
- Optional array: Use `max n` or `all`
- Enum property: Use `ObjectOneOf` or class with individuals
- Linked resource: Target class with `apiID`
- Nested object: Target class without `apiID`
- Multiple inheritance: Use class hierarchy with `apiLabel`

---

#### **docs/troubleshooting.md** - Troubleshooting
**Purpose**: Common problems and solutions

**Content**:
- "No properties found" → Check apiLabel annotations
- "Cannot infer root class" → Set rootClass explicitly
- "Missing apiID" → Add apiID property to resource classes
- Namespace issues → Check namespaceMappings
- Empty output → Verify hasResourceSchema restrictions
- Invalid security scope → Check RFC 6749 compliance

**Purpose**: Define OWL and OpenAPI terminology

toolUseResult.prompt:
Design a comprehensive end-user documentation structure for ASG (API Specification Generator) - a tool that generates OpenAPI specs from OWL ontologies.

**Context from exploration:**
- Current README.md covers basics but is dense
- Tool has: CLI options (-c config, -f ontology, -o output, -G flatten-only), extensive YAML config with inheritance, namespace mappings
- Supports 16 OWL restriction types (cardinality, value restrictions, boolean combinations)
- Maps 46 XSD datatypes to OpenAPI types
- Uses annotations: apiLabel, apiDescription, apiSummary, apiTag, apiID, security, skos:example, skos:prefLabel
- Outputs: openapi.yaml/json, ontology files (original, merged, flat), effective-config.yaml

**Requirements:**
1. Create docs/ folder structure with separate files
2. Target end-users (how to use, not how it works internally)
3. Should enable exhaustive integration test creation

**Design:**
1. List all documentation files needed with their purposes
2. Outline content for each file (sections, topics)
3. Identify which documented features will need corresponding integration tests
4. Consider a feature matrix that maps OWL constructs → OpenAPI output

Provide a detailed documentation plan.

content.text:
I now have a comprehensive understanding of the ASG tool. Let me provide the detailed documentation plan.

---

## Comprehensive Documentation Plan for ASG (API Specification Generator)

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, I have identified all features, configuration options, OWL restriction types, datatype mappings, annotations, and output formats. Below is a detailed documentation structure designed for end-users.

---

### 1. Documentation Files Structure

```
docs/
├── index.md                           # Landing page / overview
├── getting-started.md                 # Quick start guide
├── installation.md                    # Prerequisites and installation
├── cli-reference.md                   # Command-line interface reference
├── configuration/
│   ├── index.md                       # Configuration overview
│   ├── config-reference.md            # Complete YAML config reference
│   ├── config-inheritance.md          # extends and variable substitution
│   ├── templates.md                   # OpenAPI templates customization
│   └── security.md                    # Security configuration
├── ontology-design/
│   ├── index.md                       # Ontology requirements overview
│   ├── structure.md                   # Required ontology structure
│   ├── annotations.md                 # All supported annotations
│   ├── restrictions.md                # OWL restrictions and their mappings
│   └── datatypes.md                   # XSD datatype mappings
├── mapping-reference/
│   ├── index.md                       # Mapping overview
│   ├── owl-to-openapi-matrix.md       # Feature matrix: OWL → OpenAPI
│   ├── data-properties.md             # Data property mapping details
│   ├── object-properties.md           # Object property mapping details
│   └── taxonomy-properties.md         # Taxonomy/enum mapping details
├── output/
│   ├── index.md                       # Output files overview
│   ├── openapi-structure.md           # Generated OpenAPI structure
│   └── extensions.md                  # Custom x-grl-* extensions
├── examples/
│   ├── minimal-example.md             # Minimal working example
│   ├── full-example.md                # Complete real-world example
│   └── common-patterns.md             # Common OWL patterns and their output
├── troubleshooting.md                 # Common issues and solutions
└── glossary.md                        # OWL/OpenAPI terminology
```

---

### 2. Detailed Content Outline for Each File

#### **docs/index.md** - Landing Page
**Purpose**: High-level overview and navigation

**Content**:
- What is ASG
- Key capabilities summary
- When to use ASG
- Quick navigation to main sections
- Version compatibility (OWL API 5.1.20, OpenAPI 3.1)

---

#### **docs/getting-started.md** - Quick Start Guide
**Purpose**: Get users running in 5 minutes

**Content**:
1. Minimal prerequisites (Java 21+)
2. Download/build instructions (one command)
3. Simplest possible config file example
4. Run command
5. Expected output explanation
6. Link to full examples

---

#### **docs/installation.md** - Installation
**Purpose**: Complete setup instructions

**Content**:
- **Prerequisites**:
  - Java 21+
  - Maven 3.6.3+ (if building)
  - OWL ontology file
  - Optional: XML catalog file

- **Installation Methods**:
  - Download pre-built JAR
  - Build from source (`mvn clean package`)
  - Maven dependency integration
  - Maven plugin integration (exec-maven-plugin example)

- **JAR Files Produced**:
  - `asg-<version>-jar-with-dependencies.jar` - Standalone executable
  - `asg-<version>.jar` - Library JAR

---

#### **docs/cli-reference.md** - CLI Reference
**Purpose**: Complete command-line documentation

**Content**:
| Option | Long Form | Required | Description |
|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `-c` | `--config` | Yes | Configuration file path |
| `-f` | `--ontology-file` | No | Override ontology from config |
| `-o` | `--output-dir` | No | Override output directory from config |
| `-G` | `--no-generate` | No | Stop after flattening ontology (debug mode) |

**Examples**:
```bash
# Basic usage
java -jar asg-*-jar-with-dependencies.jar -c config.yaml

# Override ontology
java -jar asg-*-jar-with-dependencies.jar -c config.yaml -f my-ontology.owl

# Debug mode - only flatten
java -jar asg-*-jar-with-dependencies.jar -c config.yaml -G
```

---

#### **docs/configuration/index.md** - Configuration Overview
**Purpose**: Configuration system introduction

**Content**:
- Configuration file format (YAML)
- Inheritance mechanism overview
- Variable substitution (`${configDir}`)
- Default values (from `default.config.yaml`)
- Path resolution rules (relative to config file location)

---

#### **docs/configuration/config-reference.md** - Complete Config Reference
**Purpose**: Exhaustive reference of all config options

**Content** (organized by section):

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `name` | string | Yes | `default_project` | API identifier (alphanumeric + underscore only) |
| `ontologies` | list[URI] | Yes | - | List of OWL ontology files/URIs |
| `outputDir` | path | Yes | `outputs` | Output directory for generated files |
| `rootClass` | IRI | No | Auto-detect | IRI of root API class |
| `catalogFile` | path | No | - | XML catalog for ontology imports |
| `enforceRequiredness` | boolean | No | false | Add `required` based on cardinality |
| `extends` | path | No | - | Parent config to inherit from |
| `namespaceMappings` | map | No | - | Namespace flattening rules |
| `securitySchemeName` | string | No | Auto-detect | OAuth2 security scheme name |
| `defaultSecurityTemplate` | string | No | `{action}:{api}:{model}` | Security scope pattern (no annotation) |
| `securityTemplate` | string | No | `{action}:{api}:{security}` | Security scope pattern (with annotation) |
| `securityActionMappings` | map | No | See defaults | HTTP method to action mapping |
| `templates.openapi` | OpenAPI | No | - | Base OpenAPI template |
| `templates.collectionPathItem` | PathItem | No | - | Template for collection endpoints |
| `templates.singlePathItem` | PathItem | No | - | Template for single-item endpoints |

**Default Security Action Mappings**:
```yaml
securityActionMappings:
  GET: read
  HEAD: read
  POST: write
  PUT: write
  PATCH: write
  DELETE: write
```

---

#### **docs/configuration/config-inheritance.md** - Config Inheritance
**Purpose**: Explain extends mechanism and variable substitution

**Content**:
- **`extends` field**: How configs inherit from parent configs
- **Merge behavior**: Lists concatenate, maps merge (child wins)
- **Variable substitution**: `${configDir}` resolves to config file directory
- **Resolution order**: Child → Parent → default.config.yaml
- **Example**: Multi-environment setup with base config

---

#### **docs/configuration/templates.md** - OpenAPI Templates
**Purpose**: Customizing generated OpenAPI output

**Content**:
- **templates.openapi**: Base OpenAPI document structure
  - info, servers, components, externalDocs
  - How generated content merges

- **templates.collectionPathItem**: Template for `GET /Resource`, `POST /Resource`
  - Default responses (400, 401, 403, 406, 429, 500)

- **templates.singlePathItem**: Template for `GET/PUT/PATCH/DELETE /Resource/{id}`
  - Default responses (401, 403, 404, 406, 429, 500)

- **Merge rules**: Template provides defaults, generated content overlays

---

#### **docs/configuration/security.md** - Security Configuration
**Purpose**: OAuth2 and API key security configuration

**Content**:
- **Security scheme types**: OAuth2, API Key
- **Security templates**: Placeholders `{api}`, `{action}`, `{model}`, `{security}`
- **Per-resource security**: Using `security` annotation on `hasResourceSchema`
- **Scope generation**: How scopes are derived from templates
- **OAuth2 scope RFC compliance**: RFC 6749 section 3.3

---

#### **docs/ontology-design/index.md** - Ontology Requirements Overview
**Purpose**: Summary of what makes an ontology "ASG-ready"

**Content**:
- Required base ontology import (GRL base)
- API Root class requirement
- hasResourceSchema and hasAPI properties
- Annotation properties fragment
- apiLabel filtering mechanism

---

#### **docs/ontology-design/structure.md** - Required Ontology Structure
**Purpose**: Detailed structural requirements

**Content**:
- **API Root Class**: Entry point linking to API specification
- **Specification Entry subclass**: Named with version (e.g., `EmploymentAPIV1.0.1`)
- **hasAPI property**: Links API Root to specification class
- **hasResourceSchema property**: Defines resources to generate
  - Exactly/min/max cardinality patterns
  - Multiple resources from single root
- **apiID property**: Required on each resource class for REST identity

**Diagram**:
```
APIRoot
    └── subClassOf: hasAPI some EmploymentAPIV1.0.1
                    └── subClassOf: hasResourceSchema exactly 1 Employee
                                    hasResourceSchema exactly 1 Employer
```

---

#### **docs/ontology-design/annotations.md** - Supported Annotations
**Purpose**: Complete list of all annotation properties recognized

**Content**:

| Annotation | IRI Prefix | Purpose | Applied To |
|------------|-----------|---------|------------|
| `apiLabel` | GRL | Filter axioms for this API version | SubClassOf axioms |
| `apiDescription` | GRL | Full description for paths | Resource classes |
| `apiSummary` | GRL | Short summary for paths | Resource classes |
| `apiTag` | GRL | Grouping tags | Resource classes |
| `apiDisplayLabel` | GRL | Human-readable title | Classes/properties |
| `apiID` | GRL | Marks class as having REST identity | Resource classes |
| `security` | GRL | Security groups for resource | hasResourceSchema axioms |
| `skos:prefLabel` | SKOS | Preferred display label | Classes/properties |
| `skos:definition` | SKOS | Description fallback | Classes/properties |
| `skos:example` | SKOS | Example values for properties | Data properties, axioms |
| `rdfs:comment` | RDFS | Description fallback | Classes/properties |
| `prov:definition` | PROV | Description fallback | Classes/properties |

**Priority order for descriptions**: apiDescription > skos:definition > rdfs:comment > prov:definition

**Priority order for titles**: apiDisplayLabel > skos:prefLabel > IRI short form

---

#### **docs/ontology-design/restrictions.md** - OWL Restrictions Reference
**Purpose**: All 17 supported restriction types and their OpenAPI mapping

**Content**:

##### Object Property Restrictions

| OWL Restriction | OpenAPI Mapping | Example |
|-----------------|-----------------|---------|
| `ObjectSomeValuesFrom` | Array with `minItems: 1` | `hasAddress some Address` → array, required |
| `ObjectAllValuesFrom` | Type constraint only | `hasAddress only Address` → typed, not required |
| `ObjectMinCardinality(n)` | `minItems: n`, required if n>=1 | `hasPhone min 1` → minItems=1, required |
| `ObjectMaxCardinality(n)` | `maxItems: n` | `hasPhone max 3` → maxItems=3 |
| `ObjectExactCardinality(n)` | `minItems: n`, `maxItems: n` | `hasPhone exactly 2` → both set |
| `ObjectHasValue` | Reference to specific individual | `status value Active` |
| `ObjectOneOf` | Enum from named individuals | `oneOf(Active, Inactive)` → enum |
| `ObjectUnionOf` | `anyOf` composed schema | `Address or Location` → anyOf |
| `ObjectIntersectionOf` | `allOf` composed schema | `Person and Employee` → allOf |
| `ObjectComplementOf` | `not` schema | `not Manager` → not |

##### Data Property Restrictions

| OWL Restriction | OpenAPI Mapping | Example |
|-----------------|-----------------|---------|
| `DataSomeValuesFrom` | Array with `minItems: 1` | `hasEmail some string` → array |
| `DataAllValuesFrom` | Type constraint only | `hasEmail only string` → typed |
| `DataMinCardinality(n)` | `minItems: n` | `hasTag min 1` → minItems=1 |
| `DataMaxCardinality(n)` | `maxItems: n` | `hasTag max 5` → maxItems=5 |
| `DataExactCardinality(n)` | Both min/max | `hasCode exactly 1` → scalar |
| `DataHasValue` | `default` value | `status value "active"` → default |
| `DataOneOf` | `enum` values | `oneOf("A", "B", "C")` → enum |

##### Cardinality and Array Rules

- Cardinality = 1: Scalar property
- Cardinality > 1 or `someValuesFrom`: Array property
- `minCardinality >= 1` or `someValuesFrom`: Required property (when `enforceRequiredness: true`)

---

#### **docs/ontology-design/datatypes.md** - XSD Datatype Mappings
**Purpose**: Complete XSD to OpenAPI type mapping reference

**Content**:

| XSD Datatype | OpenAPI Type | OpenAPI Format | Notes |
|--------------|--------------|----------------|-------|
| `string` | string | - | Default |
| `boolean` | boolean | - | |
| `integer` | integer | - | |
| `int` | integer | - | |
| `long` | integer | int64 | |
| `short` | integer | - | |

| `dateTimeStamp` | string | date-time | |

| `time` | string | - | |
| `duration` | string | - | ISO 8601 duration |
| `anyURI` | string | uri | |
| `base64Binary` | string | byte | |
| `hexBinary` | string | - | |
| `language` | string | - | |
| `token` | string | - | |
| `normalizedString` | string | - | |
| `NMTOKEN` | string | - | |
| `Name` | string | - | |
| `NCName` | string | - | |
| `ID` | string | - | |
| `IDREF` | string | - | |
| `IDREFS` | string | - | |
| `ENTITY` | string | - | |
| `ENTITIES` | string | - | |
| `NOTATION` | string | - | |
| `QName` | string | - | |
| `anySimpleType` | string | - | |
| `anyType` | string | - | |
| `Literal` | string | - | RDF Literal |

| `gDay` | string | - | |
